The Bill of Rights
The Constitution has 27 amendments in total.
Checks and balances
The President of the United States is in charge of the executive branch.
The two legislative bodies of the US Congress are the Senate and the House of Representatives.
The Supreme Court is the highest court in the US.

Thomas Jefferson wrote the Declaration of Independence. He was the principal author, drafted by a committee of five that also included John Adams, Benjamin Franklin, Roger Sherman, and Robert R. Livingston, but Jefferson was responsible for composing the original draft.
The Declaration of Independence was adopted on July 4, 1776.

The US flag has 13 stripes because they represent the original 13 colonies that declared independence from Great Britain in 1776 and became the first states of the United States. These 13 colonies were Connecticut, Delaware, Georgia, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, North Carolina, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, and Virginia.
